data.title: Analysis: XRP falls below the "rising wedge" support and may face potential callback risks in the near future
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News XRP has broken the "rising wedge" pattern in the technical chart, indicating that the recent rebound of $1.60 may have ended and the sellers have regained control of the market. This pattern is composed of highs and lows that gradually converge, which usually indicates that the upward momentum is weakening. If the price falls below the lower track, it may trigger a further decline. XRP has now broken through the Ichimoku cloud chart support, which strengthens the bearish signal. If it fails to break through the recent high of $2.18, the price may fall back to the $1.6 support level. (CoinDesk)

data.title: XRP Faces Bearish Outlook as Rising Wedge Pattern Emerges
data.description: According to Odaily, XRP has formed a 'rising wedge' pattern on its technical chart, indicating that the recent rally to $1.60 may have concluded, with sellers regaining control of the market. This pattern, characterized by converging highs and lows, typically signals a weakening upward momentum. If the price falls below the lower boundary, it could lead to further declines. Currently, XRP has dropped below the Ichimoku cloud support, reinforcing the bearish signal. If it fails to surpass the recent high of $2.18, there is a risk of the price retreating to the $1.60 support level.

data.title: Legendary Trader Invalidates Bitcoin Reversal: BTC Price Bear Trap In Making?
data.description:
Legendary trader Peter Brandt has shared a new take on the current state of Bitcoin (BTC) price. This comes after the leading coin recently performed a major breakout in the year. Notably, Bitcoin surpassed a downward trendline that limited the price all through January, February, and March
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/04/16/legendary-trader-invalidates-bitcoin-reversal-btc-price-bear-trap-in-making/

data.title: China selling seized crypto to top up coffers as economy slows: Report
data.description:
Local governments in China are reportedly seeking ways to offload seized crypto while facing challenges due to the country’s ban on crypto trading and exchanges. The lack of rules around how authorities should handle seized crypto has spawned “inconsistent and opaque approaches” that some fear could foster corruption, lawyers told Reuters for an April 16 report. Chinese local governments are using private companies to sell seized cryptocurrencies in offshore markets in exchange for cash to replenish public coffers, Reuters reported, citing transaction and court documents.  The local governments reportedly held approximately 15,000 Bitcoin (BTC) worth $1
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/china-selling-seized-crypto-top-up-coffers-reuters?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Data: Bitget's derivatives market share rose to 13.25%, ranking fourth in the world's CEX
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: CoinDesk's latest March exchange report shows that Binance ranks first in global derivatives trading volume with a market share of 39.66%. Bitget's market share has increased to 13.25%, an increase of 3% from the fourth quarter of 2024, ranking fourth among global centralized exchanges.

data.title: Cathie Wood Calls It 'Financial Services Revolution' After Ark Analyst Highlights DeFi's Dominance Over Traditional Banking System
data.description:
